message:
fix(ufunc,linalg): handle zero-length axis in nan-reduction functions and fix batch_solve test indentation

- Guard nan_fill_for_axis against division by zero when axis_len == 0
- Add nan_empty_axis_result helper returning NaN-filled output for
  empty-axis reductions, matching NumPy behavior
- Add zero-axis guards to nanmean, nanvar, nanmin, nanmax, nanargmin,
  nanargmax so they return appropriate NaN/zero results for shape [0, N]
  inputs instead of panicking on division by zero
- Add nansum_empty_axis_returns_zero_identities, nanmean_empty_axis_returns_nan_lanes,
  and nanvar_empty_axis_returns_nan_lanes tests
- Fix batch_solve test indentation: move from module-level to inside
  mod tests block where it belongs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
